data.title: Abbott Laboratories declares $0.59 dividend
data.description:
• Abbott Laboratories (NYSE:ABT) declares $0.59/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 1.75%
  • Payable Nov. 17; for shareholders of record Oct. 15; ex-div Oct. 15.
  • See ABT Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Platinum Analytics Cayman prices $8M IPO, to begin trading on Nasdaq
data.description:
• Platinum Analytics Cayman (PLTS) priced its initial public offering at $4/share, offering 2M Class A ordinary shares for $8M in gross proceeds.
  • The shares are approved for listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market and will begin trading on September 19, 2025, under the ticker “PLTS.”
  • Underwriters have a 45-day option to purchase up to 300,000 additional shares to cover over-allotments.
  • The IPO is expected to close on September 22, 2025.
  • Net proceeds will be allocated as follows: ~$2.7M (40%) for R&D, ~$2.1M (30%) for marketing and sales expansion, and ~$2.0M (30%) for IT and license procurement.
  • PLTS provides FX trading software, data analytics, and tech solutions, with a focus on Asia and emerging markets.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: M/I Homes expands credit facility to $900M, extends maturity to 2030
data.description:
• M/I Homes (MHO) amended its credit agreement, increasing borrowing capacity from $650M to $900M.
  • The maturity date of the credit facility has been extended to September 2030.
  • The company holds $800M in cash and reported a negative net debt-to-capital ratio of 3% as of June 30, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: National Bank of Canada to redeem all Series 1 Limited Recourse Capital Notes
data.description:
• National Bank of Canada (TSX:NA:CA) will redeem all $500M of its 4.300% Limited Recourse Capital Notes Series 1 and all 500,000 Series 44 Preferred Shares on Nov. 15, 2025.
  • Series 1 Limited Recourse Capital Notes will be redeemed at a price equal to the principal amount of the notes, plus any accrued and unpaid interest up to, but excluding, the redemption date.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Klaviyo rises after rating upgrade at Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Shares of Klaviyo (NYSE:KVYO) rose about 6% premarket on Friday after Morgan Stanley upgraded the stock to Overweight from Equal-weight.

The firm also raised the price target on the shares to $50 from $40.

"We see Klaviyo positioned to sustain durable 20%+ growth as its market opportunity expands from email marketing to a broader CRM [Customer Relationship Management] platform for B2C [Business-to-Consumer] businesses," said analysts led by Elizabeth Porter.

The analysts noted that while it is early to size with certainty, Klaviyo management's commentary quantifying Service opportunity 'as large' as Marketing suggests Service could drive about $1.3B cumulative revenues through 2030.

In the near term, conservative forecasts position Klaviyo for estimate revisions, while a differentiated pricing model should insulate seat-based risks plaguing Software as a Service, or SaaS, multiples, the analysts added.

"Following heavy investment in product & GTM [go-to-market] to better serve a diverse customer set, we see Klaviyo poised to deliver ~100/~200 bps margin expansion in FY26/FY27," said Porter and her team.

data.title: Trump and Xi start phone call with trade, TikTok, Nvidia and more on the agenda
data.description:
President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping have started their phone call, the Chinese Embassy in U.S. said. 

The call was scheduled to start at 9 am ET; however, media reports suggest that it began early at 8 am ET, citing the White House. 

The two countries have a lot to cover, and if everything goes to schedule, a trip by Trump to China could happen soon, marking the first American president to visit the country since 2017.

Some of the topics that are in the forefront include a deal for TikTok, the antitrust investigation by Beijing on Nvidia (NVDA), and the tariff policies. 

On trade, Washington and Beijing had agreed to extend their trade truce for another 90 days in early August after a tit-for-tat tariff battle spiraled out of control.

Potential aircraft orders from Boeing (BA), as well as jet engine components from suppliers like GE Aerospace (GE), Honeywell (HON), and Collins Aerospace (RTX), will likely be discussed, as well as key rare earth elements. 

Here are some China ETFs: (KWEB), (PGJ), (CQQQ), (FXI), (GXC), (MCHI), (FLCH), (CNYA), (ASHR), and (YINN).

This is a developing story, check back for updates

data.title: Ex-Fed official Bullard backs FOMC's 25-bp cut this week
data.description:
James Bullard, the former St. Louis Fed president and a contender to be the next Federal Reserve Chair, said he wouldn't have supported a 50-basis-point rate cut if he were voting at Wednesday's Federal Open Market Committee.

"I thought the Fed's decision was a good one," he said in an interview on Bloomberg Television. When asked if he would have voted for a 50-bp cut, he said, "No, I don't think so."  Bullard said he expects a "sequence of three moves in a row through the end of the year."

At the FOMC meeting, newly-seated Fed Governor Stephen Miran was the only member to disagree with the central bank's move to lower its benchmark rate by 25 bps to 4.00%-4.25%. Instead, he preferred a 50-bp cut.

A total of 75 bps of easing by year's end would constitute a "significant" move, while leaving the Fed with "optionality" entering 2026, Bullard said. For the dovish members of the FOMC, 50 basis points of cuts in September and October would be "almost as good" as a half percentage point in September, he added.

data.title: Dream Unlimited renews share buyback program
data.description:
• Dream Unlimited (TSX:DRM:CA) renewed its normal course issuer bid for one year, starting September 23, 2025.
  • The company can repurchase up to 2.32M Class A subordinate voting shares, representing 10% of its public float.
  • The bid will end on September 22, 2026, or earlier if the maximum shares are repurchased.
  • Dream repurchased 264,899 shares under its current bid at an average price of $18.75, totaling ~$5M.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Intel slips as Citi downgrades after Nvidia deal, citing valuation, foundry concerns
data.description:
• Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) was in focus on Friday after Citi downgraded the chipmaker following its deal with Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA).
  • Shares fell 0.7% in premarket trading.
  • “We expect minimal improvement for Intel as better graphics won't make Intel's CPU better than AMD's given the processor is the main performance driver,” Citi analyst Christopher Danely wrote in a note to clients. “In addition, we don't expect much from the AI product given the small [total addressable market] ($1 to $2 billion) and our belief that the stock is pricing in success in its leading-edging foundry business, which we believe has minimal chance to succeed.”
  • Danely lowered his rating on Intel to Sell from Neutral, but upped his price target to $29 from $24.
  • Additionally, with Intel shares up roughly 50% since early August, Danely said investors are likely pricing in a deal for Intel's foundry. “We disagree given our belief that Intel's foundry is years behind [Taiwan Semiconductor] (TSM),” Danely added.

data.title: GBT Technologies to acquire Two Hands Corporation
data.description:
• GBT Technologies (OTCPK:GTCH) has authorized a non-binding indication of interest to acquire all the issued and outstanding shares of Two Hands Corporation (OTCPK:TWOH).
  • The proposed offer contemplates consideration of $0.00625 per share, payable in a to be determined combination of cash and GBT shares, in a future combination of consideration components to be determined in final negotiations between the parties.
  • This proposal includes the assumption by GBT of all outstanding debt obligations of Two Hands.
